meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Errors
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Use this intermediate guide to build a robust portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to master real-world app development.
content.answer_block: Debugging is not a wall; it is a skill gap. By shifting from 'fixing code' to 'building architecture' via portfolio projects, you learn to instrument your own systems. Use Antigravity to visualize logic flows and Claude Code to automate recursive debugging, turning every error into a repeatable architectural lesson.
content.intro:
You stare at the terminal, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat monitor for a project that just died. The stack trace is a cryptic language you don't speak, and the frustration of being stuck is the silent killer of every promising student developer's career. You aren't failing; you are simply missing the tooling bridge between theory and production.

Portfolio building is your escape velocity. By treating your code as an evolving product rather than a homework assignment, you force yourself to master the infrastructure of reliability. We are going to transform your debugging process from a guessing game into a systematic engineering discipline using Antigravity and Claude Code.

steps.title: Architectural Visualization
steps.description: Map your application state before writing a single line of code to catch logic bugs early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase architecture and generate a Mermaid diagram of the data flow between the API layer and the state management store.

steps.title: Automated Error Triage
steps.description: Use AI to classify errors into syntax, logic, or architectural debt categories.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this error stack trace. Categorize it as a logic error or dependency conflict and suggest the specific module responsible.

steps.title: Test-Driven Development (TDD) Implementation
steps.description: Write tests that define success, ensuring your portfolio project remains robust.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of unit tests for this function that covers edge cases including null inputs and asynchronous timeout failures.

steps.title: Refactoring for Maintainability
steps.description: Transform messy, bug-prone code into clean, modular components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this component to follow clean code principles, reducing cyclomatic complexity while maintaining existing functionality.

steps.title: Production-Ready Deployment
steps.description: Finalize the build by implementing logging and observability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest production-grade logging patterns for this application to ensure future bugs are easily traceable.

pro_tips: Treat your console.log statements as a 'flight recorder'—always include a timestamp and context string for easier parsing.
When stuck, force yourself to explain the code aloud to an imaginary rubber duck; the AI will catch the logical disconnects faster.
Build 'debug mode' into your UI that exposes state variables, making visual debugging 10x faster than checking the terminal.
Version control isn't just for saving; use git bisect to pinpoint exactly which commit introduced the bug.
Prioritize building small, standalone 'micro-apps' for your portfolio instead of one giant monolith; it isolates bugs and builds confidence.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify, document the specific bugs you solved in a README.md file, and share your repository with a peer for a code review.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs define your limits. Start building the portfolio that proves your engineering capability to the world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
